audit_folder_structure_guide.overview: Comprehensive guide for creating and managing organized audit folder structures. Every audit must create a well-organized folder hierarchy to ensure all artifacts are properly stored, easily accessible, and can coexist with other audits.

standard_structure.description: The standard audit folder structure organizes all audit outputs into logical categories. This structure is created automatically at the start of each audit.
standard_structure.hierarchy: audits/ └── [ObjectStoreName]_[YYYY-MM-DD]/ ├── audit_metadata.json # Audit context and tracking ├── README.md # Navigation guide and overview │ ├── reports/ # Final reports for stakeholders │ ├── Repository_Audit_Report.md │ └── Executive_Summary.md │ ├── data/ # Raw data and exports │ ├── class_inventory.csv │ ├── property_matrix.csv │ ├── document_counts.csv │ └── integration_points.json │ ├── analysis/ # Detailed analysis by area │ ├── class_analysis.md │ ├── property_analysis.md │ └── folder_analysis.md │ └── recommendations/ # Actionable recommendations ├── consolidation_plans.md ├── cleanup_actions.md └── implementation_roadmap.md

folder.name: audits
folder.purpose: Root folder for all repository audits
folder.contents: Subfolders for each audit, named by Object Store and date

folder.name: [ObjectStoreName]_[YYYY-MM-DD]
folder.naming: - ObjectStoreName: Name of the audited Object Store (e.g., "OS1", "ProductionOS") - YYYY-MM-DD: Audit date in ISO format (e.g., "2026-05-18") - Example: "OS1_2026-05-18"
folder.contents: All artifacts for this specific audit
folder.benefits: - Easy identification of which Object Store was audited - Date stamp enables version tracking - Multiple audits can coexist without conflicts - Clear separation between different audit runs

folder.name: reports
folder.purpose: Final reports for stakeholders and management
folder.contents: - Repository_Audit_Report.md: Comprehensive detailed report - Executive_Summary.md: High-level summary for executives
folder.characteristics: - Polished, presentation-ready documents - Focus on findings and recommendations - Minimal technical jargon - Clear action items

folder.name: data
folder.purpose: Raw data exports and structured data files
folder.contents: - class_inventory.csv: Complete class list with statistics - property_matrix.csv: Property usage across classes - document_counts.csv: Document counts per class - integration_points.json: Integration property mapping
folder.audience: Data analysts, technical teams, reporting tools
folder.characteristics: - Machine-readable formats (CSV, JSON) - Structured data for analysis - Can be imported into spreadsheets or databases - Supports custom reporting and visualization

folder.name: analysis
folder.purpose: Detailed technical analysis by audit area
folder.contents: - class_analysis.md: In-depth class findings and patterns - property_analysis.md: Property usage, issues, and optimization - folder_analysis.md: Folder structure and organization assessment
folder.characteristics: - Technical depth and detail - Specific examples and evidence - Analysis methodology explained - Technical recommendations

folder.name: recommendations
folder.contents: - consolidation_plans.md: Detailed class consolidation strategies - cleanup_actions.md: Step-by-step cleanup tasks - implementation_roadmap.md: Phased implementation plan
folder.audience: Project managers, implementation teams, change managers
folder.characteristics: - Action-oriented content - Prioritized recommendations - Effort estimates - Expected benefits - Implementation steps

file.name: audit_metadata.json
file.purpose: Track audit context, status, and metadata
file.example: { "object_store": "OS1", "audit_date": "2026-05-18T18:00:00Z", "audit_type": "full", "auditor": "Bob Repository Auditor", "scope": "Comprehensive repository audit covering all aspects", "status": "completed", "completion_date": "2026-05-18T19:30:00Z", "statistics": { "total_classes": 91, "total_properties": 450, "total_documents": 5000, "high_priority_issues": 5, "medium_priority_issues": 12, "low_priority_issues": 8 }, "artifacts": [ "reports/Repository_Audit_Report.md", "reports/Executive_Summary.md", "data/class_inventory.csv", "data/property_matrix.csv", "analysis/class_analysis.md", "recommendations/consolidation_plans.md" ] }

file.name: README.md
file.contents: - Audit overview and objectives - Quick links to all reports and artifacts - Summary of key findings - Navigation guide to folder structure - How to use the audit results
file.benefits: - Serves as entry point for audit results - Provides context for all artifacts - Makes navigation easy for new users - Documents audit purpose and scope

step.details: - Extract from MCP server configuration if available - Ask user if not determinable - Use clear, descriptive name (e.g., "OS1", "ProductionOS", "TestEnvironment")
- Format: [ObjectStoreName]_[YYYY-MM-DD] - Use current date in ISO format - Example: "OS1_2026-05-18"
- Set initial status to "in_progress" - Include audit date, type, scope - Will be updated on completion
- Add audit overview - Include folder structure explanation - Add placeholders for key findings - Will be updated as audit progresses
- Reports go in reports/ - Data exports go in data/ - Analysis documents go in analysis/ - Recommendations go in recommendations/
- Set status to "completed" - Add completion_date - Add summary statistics - List all generated artifacts

practice.title: Always Create Structure First
practice.description: Create the complete folder structure at the start of the audit, before generating any content. This ensures all artifacts have a proper home.

practice.title: Use Consistent Naming
practice.description: Always use the same file names across different audits. This makes it easy to compare audits and find specific artifacts.

practice.title: Update Metadata Progressively
practice.description: Update audit_metadata.json as the audit progresses, not just at the end. This provides status tracking and recovery if interrupted.

practice.title: Generate README Early
practice.description: Create README.md early in the audit process and update it as findings emerge. This provides a living document that guides users through the audit.

practice.title: Separate Concerns
practice.description: Keep different types of artifacts in their designated folders. Don't mix reports with data files or analysis with recommendations.

practice.title: Include Examples in Data Files
practice.description: When generating CSV files, include header rows and a few example rows to make the data structure clear.

scenario.name: same_object_store
scenario.description: Multiple audits of the same Object Store over time
scenario.benefits: - Track changes over time - Compare before/after improvements - Monitor progress on recommendations - Identify trends and patterns

scenario.name: multiple_object_stores
scenario.benefits: - Compare different environments - Identify common issues across stores - Share best practices - Standardize approaches

issue.solution: - Verify write permissions in project directory - Check for invalid characters in Object Store name - Use execute_command with proper error handling
- Ask user for Object Store name - Provide suggestions based on common names - Use generic name like "ObjectStore" if unavailable
- Add time component: [ObjectStore]_[YYYY-MM-DD]_[HHMM] - Ask user if they want to overwrite or create new - Suggest reviewing existing audit first
